docs.title: Letter 10006
docs.author: Cassiodorus
docs.subject_summary: Book 10, Letter VI: PATRICIO V. I. QUAESTORI THEODAHADUS
docs.excerpt: How splendid it is, when everyone wants to speak, to express common things so skillfully that even the wise marvel at what they hear! We have recognized in you such strength in this art that you can persuade with grace

docs.title: Letter 97
docs.author: Theodoret of Cyrrhus
docs.subject_summary: I am delighted with your excellency's letter. My pleasure has been increased by the very religious presbyter and monk Iamblichus, who has told me of your warm zeal, your earnestness in religion, and y
docs.excerpt: … on my behalf of the most glorious and devout lord Patricius — I pronounce over you the apostolic blessing that the blessed Onesiphorus received from that holy tongue: "May the Lord grant mercy to the household of Onesiphorus, for he often refreshed me and was not ashamed of my chains.
